description: Shakes-peer blog quality rules — governance catalog for auditor checks

tiers.1: DOM or regex on structured draft

severity.HIGH: Must pass for pillars; authors can override with reason

rules.title: Primary keyword appears in the page title or H1
rules.verification_method: Look at article.title or the first <h1> and compare it to request.primary_keyword.
rules.generation_instruction: Include the exact primary keyword (or a very close variant) in the H1 / title.

rules.title: Meta title is keyword-aligned and within 45-65 characters
rules.verification_method: Count article.meta_title characters and verify it includes request.primary_keyword or a close variant.
rules.generation_instruction: Write a meta_title 45-65 chars long that leads with the primary keyword.

rules.title: Meta description is specific and within 140-165 characters
rules.verification_method: Count article.meta_description characters and check for concrete nouns or numbers, not empty hype.
rules.generation_instruction: Write a meta_description 140-165 chars that includes the primary keyword and a concrete value proposition.

rules.title: Slug is concise and keyword-relevant
rules.verification_method: Check article.slug against request.primary_keyword and keep it under 80 characters.
rules.generation_instruction: Keep slug under 80 chars, lowercase, hyphens, include the primary keyword.

rules.title: The article body has enough topical depth
rules.verification_method: Count section blocks and total visible words across article text or HTML.
rules.generation_instruction: Target the word count in BlogArticleOptions.target_word_count (defaults by post type).

rules.title: The post includes a fast-answer block near the top
rules.verification_method: Check article.quick_answer or an equivalent early answer block in the first 20% of HTML.
rules.generation_instruction: Produce 3-5 quick_answer bullets — each is a standalone, extractable answer to the primary query.

rules.title: Headings are question-led or snippet-friendly
rules.verification_method: Count H2 entries that are question-led or snippet-friendly (40% question form target).
rules.generation_instruction: Phrase at least 40% of H2 headings as questions ending with '?'.

rules.title: The post includes reader-retention elements
rules.verification_method: Look for bullets, tables, or key_takeaways that break up text.
rules.generation_instruction: Include at least one list, table, or key_takeaways block per 500 words.

rules.title: Paragraphs are concise enough for AI extraction
rules.verification_method: Compute average paragraph word count across article text or HTML.
rules.generation_instruction: Keep paragraphs 2-4 sentences, max 80 words.

rules.title: A clear next step or CTA is present
rules.verification_method: Check article.call_to_action or an anchor link marked as CTA in the HTML.
rules.generation_instruction: Emit call_to_action with a topic-relevant label and URL.

rules.title: HTML contains one semantic <article> root and exactly one <h1>
rules.verification_method: Parse HTML for a single <article> element and one <h1>.
rules.generation_instruction: Render exactly one <article> and one <h1> in the output HTML.

rules.title: Heading hierarchy is clean
rules.verification_method: Heading sequence does not skip levels (H1 → H2 → H3 only).
rules.generation_instruction: Never skip heading levels in the HTML output.

rules.title: Inline citation anchors resolve to reference targets
rules.verification_method: Every <sup class='citation'> link must resolve to a #source-* anchor.
rules.generation_instruction: Only cite source IDs that appear in the sources list.

rules.title: Primary keyword and intent are embedded as machine-readable HTML attributes
rules.verification_method: Check for data-primary-keyword and data-search-intent attributes on the <article>.
rules.generation_instruction: Emit data-primary-keyword and data-search-intent on the <article>.

rules.title: HTML includes an embedded JSON-LD script
rules.verification_method: Find <script type='application/ld+json'> in the <head>.
rules.generation_instruction: Embed the JSON-LD graph in the <head>.

rules.title: JSON-LD includes a BlogPosting node
rules.verification_method: Parse JSON-LD graph; require a node with @type BlogPosting.
rules.generation_instruction: Include BlogPosting in the JSON-LD graph with all required fields.

rules.title: Core BlogPosting fields are complete
rules.verification_method: BlogPosting has headline, author, datePublished, image, publisher, mainEntityOfPage.
rules.generation_instruction: Populate every required BlogPosting field.

rules.title: FAQ schema is present when the article contains FAQs
rules.verification_method: If article.faq has >= 3 items, JSON-LD must include FAQPage.
rules.generation_instruction: When emitting 3+ FAQ items, also emit FAQPage schema in the graph.

rules.title: Evidence sources are modeled as cited entities in JSON-LD
rules.verification_method: BlogPosting.citation[] mirrors the sources array.
rules.generation_instruction: Mirror every source as a CreativeWork in the JSON-LD graph and link from BlogPosting.citation.

rules.title: JSON-LD names the main entities or topics explicitly
rules.verification_method: BlogPosting.about or mentions includes Thing/Person/Organization nodes.
rules.generation_instruction: Include about and mentions arrays in BlogPosting schema.

rules.title: Format-specific schema is present when the format requires it
rules.verification_method: howto -> HowTo schema; comparison -> ItemList/Table; metric -> Dataset; glossary -> DefinedTerm.
rules.generation_instruction: Emit format-specific schema for the chosen post_format.

rules.title: Factual paragraphs are source-backed
rules.verification_method: Every intro/section/FAQ/conclusion paragraph has at least one citation_id.
rules.generation_instruction: Attach citation_ids to every factual paragraph.

rules.title: Provided sources are actually used
rules.verification_method: No source IDs in validation.uncited_source_ids.
rules.generation_instruction: Cite every source provided at least once; remove unused sources.

rules.title: The article contains concrete specifics, not just generic claims
rules.verification_method: Look for numbers, dates, prices, percentages, or named frameworks in the body.
rules.generation_instruction: Use specific numbers, dates, and named frameworks — not vague claims.

rules.title: Brand mentions feel integrated rather than stuffed
rules.verification_method: Brand name frequency vs word count should stay in a reasonable band.
rules.generation_instruction: Mention the brand naturally — not in every paragraph.

rules.title: The article closes with a synthesized conclusion
rules.verification_method: Check for at least one conclusion paragraph with >= 12 words.
rules.generation_instruction: End with a synthesized conclusion that restates the main takeaway, not just a CTA.

rules.title: Author is a Person entity with sameAs LinkedIn URL
rules.verification_method: Check JSON-LD BlogPosting.author for Person with sameAs containing linkedin.com.
rules.generation_instruction: Supply `author: { name, title, bio, linkedin_url }` in the request.

rules.title: Article contains first-party data signals
rules.verification_method: Match 'we found', 'our data', 'in our audit', 'we tested' + specific numbers in the body.
rules.generation_instruction: Weave every FirstPartyData finding into the body using 'we found' / 'our data' phrasing.

rules.title: Article cites named brands/products with specifics (not generic)
rules.verification_method: Count proper-noun brand mentions; flag generic phrases like 'leading companies'.
rules.generation_instruction: Use the provided named_examples verbatim — never replace with 'top brands' or similar.

rules.title: Article takes a clear editorial stance
rules.verification_method: Look for Our-Take banner or stance language without hedging.
rules.generation_instruction: State the editorial_stance claim in intro and conclusion; render an 'Our take' banner.

rules.title: Article includes an original visual (screenshot, diagram, chart)
rules.verification_method: HTML must contain original-visual markers with no unreplaced placeholders.
rules.generation_instruction: Declare at least one original_visual; replace placeholder with asset_url before publish.

rules.title: 3+ primary-tier citations are present
rules.verification_method: Count sources with authority_tier=primary; require >= 3.
rules.generation_instruction: Include at least 3 primary-tier sources (arXiv, OpenAI docs, Google Search Central, gov/edu).

rules.title: Article has visible Last Reviewed + Next Review dates
rules.verification_method: HTML shows 'Last reviewed' + 'Next review' and JSON-LD has dateModified.
rules.generation_instruction: Render the rich byline (happens automatically when `author` is provided) and set next_review_date.

rules.title: No banned AI-tell phrases in the body
rules.verification_method: String-match against the banned-phrase list (e.g., 'in today's fast-paced world').
rules.generation_instruction: Avoid all banned AI-tell phrases defined in the writer prompt.
